data.title: Ulta reigns supreme in the beauty business - analysts
data.description:
“The brands need Ulta!” says J.P. Morgan’s Christopher Horvers, and the company’s flip to positive comparable sales represents more nails in the coffin of the structural bear.

“We believe the short narrative that ULTA is structurally encroached is strongly misplaced, and we believe the pain has been largely cyclical,” Horvers adds, keeping his Overweight rating on the stock and moving his price target to $480, a 12% upside to Friday’s closing price.

Shares of Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) were driven higher on Friday and traded above its 200-day average for the first time since April on solid Q3 results that included positive comparable sales, an encouraging sign after a 2% contraction in the previous quarter.

Ulta’s (ULTA) business is performing much stronger than other specialty retailers thanks to strong traffic, and the company’s guidance is achievable even if forced to take on more promotional pricing in the first half of the year, says Citi’s Kelly Crago.

Even with the lingering threat from Sephora. The recent challenges at Kohl’s (KSS) should help “put to rest some bearish concerns on ULTA,” pertaining to competition from Sephora, Oppenheimer’s Rupesh Parikh and Erica Eiler said in Friday’s research report.

Canaccord Genuity’s Susan Anderson agrees. “We believe as Sephora’s new door count slows, Ulta continues to utilize levers to retain and win back share and continue to offer a diverse set of products that the retailer can succeed long term.” Although Kohl’s (KSS) announced plans to expand its Sephora partnership, diminished traffic to the department store could spell trouble for its cosmetics partner to Ulta’s (ULTA) benefit.

Canaccord’s November beauty survey continues to highlight that consumers generally prefer Ulta (ULTA) over Sephora for prestige beauty needs. A trend that is expected to accelerate as Sephora’s comparable sales at Kohl’s (KSS) slowed to 9% in Q3 from the 20s last year. “Not a good look on a typical retail store maturation waterfall,” Horvers says.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors are bullish on Ulta (ULTA) rating the stock as a Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating is neutral on the stock with a Hold rating.

data.title: Presidio granted second 180-day period by Nasdaq to regain compliance
data.description:
• Presidio Property Trust (NASDAQ:SQFT) said on Friday that it has been granted an additional 180-day period from Nasdaq’s Listing Qualification Department, through June 2, 2025, to regain compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • The company’s common stock continues to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “SQFT.”
  • If at any time until June 2, 2025, the closing bid price of the company’s common stock is at or above $1.00 per share for a minimum of ten consecutive trading days, Nasdaq will provide the company with written confirmation of compliance.

data.title: Lilly tops Morgan Stanley's biopharma pick list for 2025
data.description:
Morgan Stanley has chosen Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) as its top biopharma pick for 2025 and rated another nine names in the space as overweight.

The investment bank said in a note that it continues to believe “diabesity is set to become among the largest pharma categories of all time,” adding oral GLP-1 drugs such as Lilly’s (LLY) orforglipron will be needed amid ongoing supply constraints for injectable GLP-1s.

“We see an opportunity for LLY to differentiate on multiple fronts. We expect orforglipron to reach the market at least ~2 years ahead of competitor oral drugs, giving LLY a significant time-to-market advantage,” Morgan Stanley said.

The bank said it’s modeled a 2026 U.S. launch for orforglipron, with 2033 diabesity sales of around $10B, risk-adjusted by a 70% probability of success. In its bull case scenario, the bank estimates the drug can grab around 75% of the oral GLP-1 market, with unadjusted 2033 worldwide sales of $31B.

Morgan Stanley also rated large caps AbbVie (ABBV), Regeneron (REGN) and Royalty Pharma (RPRX) overweight. It added it was likewise bullish on small/mid-caps Alumis (ALMS), Biohaven (BHVN), Structure Therapeutics (GPCR), Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) and Arcus Biosciences (RCUS), rating all five overweight.

data.title: Centurion announces private placement financing
data.description:
• Centurion Minerals (TSXV:CTN:CA) on Friday announced a non-brokered private placement financing for up to $200,000 priced at $0.02 per unit.
  • The unit offering is comprised of one common share and one share purchase warrant. Each warrant shall have a term of 24 months commencing on the closing date entitling the holder to purchase one common share at a price of $0.05 for 2 years.
  • Financing proceeds are anticipated to be allocated as to 60% for working capital and general corporate activities, 30% for project exploration and 10% for corporate communications.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dynamix announces separate trading of its class A ordinary shares and warrants
data.description:
• Dynamix Corporation (NASDAQ:DYNXU) said on Friday that commencing December 9, 2024, holders of the units sold in the company’s initial public offering may elect to separately trade the Class A ordinary shares and warrants included in the units.
  • Class A ordinary shares and warrants that are separated will trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol “DYNX” and “DYNXW,” respectively.
  • Those units not separated will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol “DYNXU.”
  • No fractional warrants will be issued upon separation of the units and only whole warrants will trade.

data.title: Roblox continues its green streak for seven straight sessions
data.description:
Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) continued gains for seven straight sessions as the stock closed 0.61% higher, at $59.30 on Friday.

The online gaming platform company gained more than 19% in the last six trading sessions. The stock has gained more than 28% so far this year, in-line with 27% growth in the broader S&P 500 Index.

RBLX is up 11% over the past one month. The stock closed 8.07% up, at $58.94 on Thursday.

Analysts at Morgan Stanley upgraded Roblox (RBLX) last month to "overweight" following its better than expected earnings and said the company's success is reaching a broader audience and scaling on new platforms, which suggests growing opportunity.

They noted that trends at the company have been steadily improving since Q1, as bookings growth accelerated ~1,500 bps, and that its non-console business contributed 92% of bookings and grew 28% in Q3, nearly 10x faster than the game market in 2024 and the highest level since peak COVID in 2021.

SA Authors and sell-side analysts are bullish on the stock, rating it as a BUY. However, Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating has a cautious stance, rating the stock a HOLD, with a score of 3.37 out of 5.

Seeking Alpha analyst Selendis Research believes that despite significant user growth and revenue expansion, Roblox remains unprofitable and faces high costs, particularly from platform and developer fees, hindering positive operating income.

“Valuation analysis suggests Roblox is overvalued, with a potential 40% downside, making it prudent for investors to consider taking profits or avoiding new investments,” the analyst wrote.

data.title: Citigroup snaps six consecutive sessions of gain
data.description:
Banking giant Citigroup (NYSE:C) snapped its six-day win streak on Friday, with the stock closing the day 0.15% lower at $72.13.

C had logged six consecutive sessions of gain after the financial services firm, CFRA, said it expects banks to outperform the broader S&P 500 index next year. CFRA had highlighted Citi as a stock on its radar.

The organization stated its belief that the Trump administration will introduce new policies and reduce regulations, which could positively influence capital markets and the investment banking sector.

The stock gained 3.5% over the course of the last six trading days, with a significant rise recorded yesterday. C closed Thursday, December 5, 1.02% higher at $72.23.

"Citigroup is one of the largest banks in the U.S., and the banking sector is one of the most regulated sectors. At any point, regulations can change, which impacts Citigroup negatively, causes their capital requirements to increase, and adds compliance costs," noted Seeking Alpha author Steven Fiorillo in a research note.

"I still think that Citigroup is undervalued and should trade for more than $100 per share," said Fiorillo.

The stock has risen ~40% year-to-date, and is trading close to its 52-week high of $72.85. Short interest stood at 1.78% of the total float as of November 15, representing 33.55M shares sold short.

"The bank continues to invest internally and simplify the business, while retaining (and even growing!) market share in key segments. Once C gets to 'the other side', we expect rapid EPS expansion and continued share price appreciation towards 2030 and beyond," said SA contributor PropNotes.

"While there are some executions, regulatory, and macro risks to be aware of, we think C is an attractive investment opportunity at this point in time. Thus, we're reiterating our 'Buy' rating," the author noted.

SA authors and the sell-side analysts grade the stock as Buy.

The New York, NY-based bank found a place in the Wells Fargo "value equity list" of companies that are believed to trade at a discount to their underlying fundamentals and could have a potential to reduce or eliminate that discount.

Meanwhile, SA's Quant Rating system rates C as Hold.

"Rising credit costs, underwhelming RoTCE, and underperforming in some segments like US Personal Banking expose vulnerabilities that cannot be ignored. I think Citi's stock represents a calculated risk. However, it is not for those looking for quick, safe, and stable growth," said SA contributor Shariq Khan.

data.title: Amazon saw engagement with interactive ads climb during the Black Friday NFL game - report
data.description:
According to a Deadline report from Friday, Amazon saw a 10% rise in customer engagement with interactive ads this year during the Black Friday NFL game vs. last year’s debut edition.

The level of engagement on ads was monitored by checking the use of remotes to click and scan interactive messages, the report said. Viewership of the Kansas City Chiefs-Las Vegas Raiders game surged 41% from last year.

There was a 30% year-over-year increase in interactive ads, and about 40% of the brands present in the game were there for the first time, Deadline said, citing the tech giant's internal data points, and about 20% of all advertising featured new creative designed to debut on the game.

“The Black Friday game is changing consumer behaviors for both fans and advertisers as a new sports holiday on one of the biggest shopping days. Prime Video’s leaned-in audience came ready to engage with our Black Friday advertisers through clicks, scans, and search which resulted in our highest customer engagement on record,” Danielle Carney, who oversees Live Sports and Video Sales for Amazon Ads, told Deadline.

Amazon said interactivity in ad messages was 11x more effective in driving engagement vs. just displaying a QR code.

The report claimed Amazon has found viewers on Black Friday were 51% more likely to search for brands or products advertised on the game than viewers of the three NFL Thanksgiving Day games due to the interactive push and the post-holiday timing.

data.title: BofA turns bearish on Liberty Global; cuts rating to 'underperform'
data.description:
• Bank of America downgraded their investment rating on Liberty Global (NASDAQ:LBTYA) to "underperform" from "neutral," saying that the company's remaining strategic initiatives post the Sunrise spinoff "are less convincing" and come with more execution risk.
  • The research firm noted Liberty faces the ongoing challenge of a cable-to-fiber migration and thinks regions could adopt contrasting strategies: building in the UK but moving to full wholesale in Holland.
  • "Notwithstanding Liberty’s strategic challenges, its core assets continue to report mixed operational performance, with the UK and Belgium both expected to report negative FY EBITDA," BofA said on Friday.
  • BofA has a PT of $12.60 for LBTYA, implying a downside of 6%.
